title: quercus × robbinsii
intro: Quercus × robbinsii, also known as the Robbin's oak or the white oak hybrid, is a deciduous tree that is a natural hybrid of the white oak (Quercus alba) and the black oak (Quercus velutina). This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains tannins that have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The health benefits of Quercus × robbinsii include the potential to reduce fever, relieve diarrhea, and soothe gastrointestinal issues due to its astringent properties. Herbal preparations of Quercus × robbinsii, such as teas and tinctures, can be made from its bark and leaves, and are often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
